title: gypsophila afghanica
intro: Gypsophila afghanica, also known as Afghan baby's breath or Afghan gypsophila, is a flowering plant native to Afghanistan and the surrounding regions. It is a member of the carnation family and is known for its small white or pink flowers that bloom in clusters. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Gypsophila afghanica can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are said to have various health benefits, including improving sleep quality and reducing stress.
